The UK Space Agency sets the national direction on space.

UK Space Agency is part of the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: This report was issued in May 2026 and covers the time period 1 April 2026 to 30 April 2026 inclusive.
ordered_featured_documents.title: How we protected the UK and space in April 2026

ordered_featured_documents.summary: During The King’s visit to Bermuda, His Majesty visited the site which will be the first of the UK’s new global network of optical telescopes designed to protect the satellites that millions of people depend on every day.
ordered_featured_documents.title: The King visits site of new UK Space Agency project to safeguard satellites in an increasingly crowded space

ordered_featured_documents.summary: This report was issued in April 2026 and covers the time period 1 March 2026 to 31 March 2026 inclusive.
ordered_featured_documents.title: How we protected the UK and space in March 2026

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Today, Earth Day, invites us all to reflect on our relationship with Earth and the protection of our planet.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Earth Day: The Power of Earth Observation

ordered_featured_documents.summary: British scientists have launched a crew of microscopic worms to the International Space Station in a pioneering experiment that could help unlock the secrets of long-duration space travel - and support ambitions to reach the Moon and beyond.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Space Worms! UK scientists launch microscopic crew into orbit to support future Moon missions

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Learn more about how we’re helping protect the Earth and space from contamination
ordered_featured_documents.title: Protecting our Planet – 1 year on

ordered_featured_links.title: Find information and contact the UK Space Agency

details.body:
The Director leads the UK Space Agency, which sets the national direction on space.  

The UK Space Agency coheres policy, strategy and delivery across the whole of government, driving innovation and delivering world-class space science programmes in partnership with the UK space sector and international partners. 

The UK Space Agency is part of the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology.

details.body: The director oversees the UK Space Agency’s international relations, inspiration and outreach activities and leads on how we engage with audiences on the growing importance of space.

role_appointments.title: Professor Anu Ojha OBE - Director of Championing Space

details.body: The director oversees a portfolio of space science and exploration missions and has a responsibility for the regulation and best practice of making space activity more sustainable. This is one of three Missions and Capabilities Director roles that deliver work to achieve the National Space Strategy’s ambitions to grow the space economy.

role.title: Missions and Capabilities Director – Discovery and Sustainability

role_appointments.title: Julie Black - Missions and Capabilities Director – Discovery and Sustainability
Julie Black - Deputy Director, EO & Discovery

details.body: The director oversees the growth and potential of Earth Observation and Low Earth Orbit (LEO) capabilities, to make the UK a global leader in climate change and LEO technology. This is one of three Missions and Capabilities Director roles that deliver work to achieve the National Space Strategy’s ambitions to grow the space economy.

details.body: The director is responsible for achieving the first small satellite launch from Europe and helping the UK become the leading provider of commercial small satellite launch services (Europe) by 2030.

role_appointments.title: Matthew Archer - Missions and Capabilities Director - Launch
Matthew Archer - Deputy Director, Launch, SDA & ISAM

details.body:
The Investment Director leads the Agency’s work to catalyse investment in the UK space sector.

This role will forge strong relationships with space investors, suppliers and customers, create opportunities to bring new investment and revenue into the sector, and lead the delivery of Agency programmes.

Tania Celani - Deputy Director, International & Resilience (job share)

ordered_parent_organisations.title: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

details.body: The director oversees the UK Space Agency’s international relations, inspiration and outreach activities and leads on how we engage with audiences on the growing importance of space.

The director oversees the growth and potential of Earth Observation and Low Earth Orbit (LEO) capabilities, to make the UK a global leader in climate change and LEO technology. This is one of three Missions and Capabilities Director roles that deliver work to achieve the National Space Strategy’s ambitions to grow the space economy.

ordered_roles.title: Missions and Capabilities Director - Earth Observation and Low-Earth Orbit Assets

details.body: The director oversees the agency’s involvement with the European Space Agency on space science, technology and exploratory missions. They also work with the steering board on telecoms and navigation, and manage education, skills and outreach programs.

The director’s primary responsibility is to provide policy advice on civil space activities to the Minister for Science (and other ministers as required). They also lead on security and international communications.

The Chief Operating and Finance Officer is responsible for the UK Space Agency’s operational, financial and administrative functions.

Their responsibilities include leading the agency’s:

  • performance management regime
  • legal, commercial, recruitment and procurement matters
  • estates and facilities management, security and IT systems

They also lead the UK delegation to the Administration and Finance Committee of the European Space Agency.

ordered_roles.title: Chief Operating and Finance Officer, UK Space Agency

details.body: The director is responsible for providing strategic advice to Agency teams and leading on the work to set the Agency’s corporate plan, managing its performance, risks and finances, and developing new research and analysis.

The director’s primary responsibility is to provide policy advice on civil space activities to the Minister for Universities and Science (and other ministers as required). They also lead on security and international communications.
The UK Space Agency’s Deputy CEO for Project Delivery supports the Chief Executive with expertise in the delivery of major National projects and programmes.

ordered_roles.title: Deputy Chief Executive for Project Delivery

details.body: The Director of Regulation is responsible for the development and standing up of a new regulator for space in line with the requirements of the Space Industry Act 2018. They also lead on satellite licensing currently under the Outer Space Act 1986, and its future transition to the new UK Space Regulator. The director leads the Agency’s work with the Department for Transport and the Civil Aviation Authority on secondary legislation needed to implement the Space Industry Act.
The director is responsible for achieving the first small satellite launch from Europe and helping the UK become the leading provider of commercial small satellite launch services (Europe) by 2030.

The Deputy CEO is responsible for the delivery of all national space programmes across the UK Space Agency.

The role also deputises for the CEO in their absence, chairs the UK Space Agency Delivery Board, is the Agency’s head of profession for programme and project delivery and is a member of the UK Space Agency Steering Board.

ordered_roles.title: Deputy CEO for Programme Delivery (Job Share)

details.body: The director is responsible for providing strategic HR advice to Agency teams and leading on the work to deliver the Agency’s People Strategy, supporting delivery of priority business outcomes and enabling the Agency to achieve its ambitions for the future, both within the Civil Service and across the Space Sector.

The Investment Director leads the Agency’s work to catalyse investment in the UK space sector.

This role will forge strong relationships with space investors, suppliers and customers, create opportunities to bring new investment and revenue into the sector, and lead the delivery of Agency programmes.

The director oversees a portfolio of space science and exploration missions and has a responsibility for the regulation and best practice of making space activity more sustainable. This is one of three Missions and Capabilities Director roles that deliver work to achieve the National Space Strategy’s ambitions to grow the space economy.

ordered_roles.title: Missions and Capabilities Director – Discovery and Sustainability

details.body:
The Deputy CEO is responsible for the delivery of all national space programmes across the UK Space Agency.

The role also deputises for the CEO in their absence, chairs the UK Space Agency Delivery Board, is the Agency’s head of profession for programme and project delivery and is a member of the UK Space Agency Steering Board.
